steps.title: Recognize the x-intercepts as zeros of the polynomial
steps.content: The graph passes through the points $(-5, 0)$, $(1, 0)$, and $(4, 0)$. These are x-intercepts, which means $f(-5) = 0$, $f(1) = 0$, and $f(4) = 0$.

steps.title: Apply the Factor Theorem
steps.content:
The Factor Theorem tells us that when a zero occurs at $x = a$, the corresponding factor is $(x - a)$. From our zeros:
  • $x = -5$ gives factor $(x + 5)$
  • $x = 1$ gives factor $(x - 1)$
  • $x = 4$ gives factor $(x - 4)$
steps.formula: $$\text{If } f(a) = 0, \text{ then } (x - a) \text{ is a factor}$$

steps.title: Identify which factor is among the answer choices
steps.content: Among the factors $(x + 5)$, $(x - 1)$, and $(x - 4)$, only $(x - 1)$ appears in the answer choices. That matches choice C — C is correct.
